================================================
FILE: README.md
================================================
# paperfetcher

[![PyPI version fury.io](https://badge.fury.io/py/paperfetcher.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/paperfetcher/)
[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/endpoint.svg?url=https%3A%2F%2Factions-badge.atrox.dev%2Fpaperfetcher%2Fpaperfetcher%2Fbadge&style=flat)](https://actions-badge.atrox.dev/paperfetcher/paperfetcher/goto)
[![Website](https://img.shields.io/website?label=docs&url=https%3A%2F%2Fimg.shields.io%2Fwebsite%2Fhttps%2Fpaperfetcher.github.io%2Fpaperfetcher)](https://paperfetcher.github.io/paperfetcher)
[![GitHub license](https://badgen.net/github/license/paperfetcher/paperfetcher)](https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher/blob/master/LICENSE)
[![Open Issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-raw/paperfetcher/paperfetcher)](https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher/issues)

## About

Paperfetcher is a Python package to automate handsearching and citation searching
(snowballing) for systematic reviews. Paperfetcher works with Python 3.7+.

To learn more about Paperfetcher, visit [paperfetcher.github.io](https://paperfetcher.github.io/).

## Usage

To get started, browse the following *Getting Started* Jupyter notebooks (also in the `examples/` directory in this
repository):

1. [Getting started with handsearching](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/paperfetcher/paperfetcher/master?labpath=examples%2F01_handsearching.ipynb)
2. [Getting started with citation searching](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/paperfetcher/paperfetcher/master?labpath=examples%2F02_citation_searching.ipynb)

Once you understand the workflow, read the [Module Documentation](https://paperfetcher.github.io/paperfetcher/paperfetcher.html) to
learn more about paperfetcher's various modules, classes and functions.

## Installation

### Installation with pip

1. [Install pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installation/)
2. Install paperfetcher using pip:
```sh
pip install paperfetcher
```

### Installation from source

1. Clone this repository
2. In the repository directory, run
```sh
python setup.py install
```
or, if you have pip installed
```sh
pip install .
```

## Running tests (if installed from source)

You can run tests to make sure that paperfetcher is working correctly on your system.

1. To run integration tests, in the repository directory, run
```sh
cd tests/tests_integration
pytest
```

2. To run unit tests, in the repository directory, run
```sh
cd tests/tests_unit
pytest
```

## The Team

Paperfetcher was developed by [Akash Pallath](https://apallath.github.io) at the [University of Pennsylvania](https://www.upenn.edu) and [Qiyang Zhang](https://qiyangzh.github.io) at the [Johns Hopkins University](https://www.jhu.edu).



================================================
FILE: bumpversion.py
================================================
import os
import argparse


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("release_type", help="Which version to bump (major, minor, revision)")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    with open("VERSION", "r") as vh:
        version = vh.read()

    version_list = [int(v) for v in version.strip().split('.')]

    if args.release_type == "major":
        version_list[0] += 1
        version_list[1] = 0
        version_list[2] = 0
    elif args.release_type == "minor":
        version_list[1] += 1
        version_list[2] = 0
    elif args.release_type == "revision":
        version_list[2] += 1
    else:
        raise ValueError("Undefined release type.")

    new_version = ".".join([str(v) for v in version_list])
    print("Updating version from %s to %s" % (version, new_version))

    with open("VERSION", "w") as vh:
        vh.write(new_version)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

================================================
FILE: release.sh
================================================
#!/bin/bash
# Call this script with argument for bumpverison.py

# exit on first error
set -e

# Bump version
python bumpversion.py $1

# Create distribution
python -m build

# Git commit and tag
git add .
git commit -m "$1 version update"

version=`cat VERSION`
git tag -a v${version} -m "$1 version update"

git push origin master
git push origin --tags

# Push distribution to pypi
python -m twine upload --skip-existing dist/*



================================================
FILE: setup.py
================================================
from setuptools import setup

with open("README.md", "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
    long_description = fh.read()

with open("VERSION", "r") as vh:
    version = vh.read()
version = version.strip()

setup(name='paperfetcher',
      python_requires='>3.7',
      version=version,
      author='Akash Pallath',
      author_email='apallath@seas.upenn.edu',
      description='Python package to mine papers for systematic reviews.',
      long_description=long_description,
      long_description_content_type="text/markdown",
      url='https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher',
      packages=['paperfetcher'],
      install_requires=[
          'openpyxl',
          'pandas',
          'pytest',
          'requests',
          'rispy',
          'stqdm',
          'tqdm',
      ],)

  <section id="paperfetcher">
<h1>paperfetcher<a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h1>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Release</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>1.1.0</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p>Paperfetcher is a Python package to automate handsearching and citation searching
(snowballing) for systematic reviews. To learn more about Paperfetcher, visit
<a class="reference external" href="https://paperfetcher.github.io/">paperfetcher.github.io</a>.</p>
<p>Paperfetcher works with Python 3.7+.</p>
<p>You can either install paperfetcher using <a class="reference external" href="https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/">pip</a>, or alternatively, clone the source code from
Github and install it from source.</p>
<section id="installation-with-pip">
<h2>Installation with pip<a class="headerlink" href="#installation-with-pip" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p><a class="reference external" href="https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/getting-started/">Install pip</a></p></li>
</ol>
<ol class="arabic simple" start="2">
<li><p>Install paperfetcher using pip</p></li>
</ol>
<p>Open a terminal window and run</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>pip install paperfetcher
</pre></div>
</div>
<p><strong>OR</strong></p>
</section>
<section id="installation-from-source-requires-git">
<h2>Installation from source (requires git)<a class="headerlink" href="#installation-from-source-requires-git" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p><strong>Source code</strong> is available from
<a class="reference external" href="https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher">https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher</a></p>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>Obtain the sources with <a class="reference external" href="https://git-scm.com/">git</a></p></li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>git clone https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher.git
</pre></div>
</div>
<ol class="arabic simple" start="2">
<li><p>Install package</p></li>
</ol>
<p>Open a terminal window in the paperfetcher directory and run</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>python setup.py install
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="running-tests-if-installed-from-source">
<h2>Running tests (if installed from source)<a class="headerlink" href="#running-tests-if-installed-from-source" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>You can run tests to make sure that paperfetcher is working correctly on your system.</p>
<p>To run unit tests:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nb">cd</span> tests/tests_unit
pytest
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To run integration tests:</p>
<div class="highlight-bash notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nb">cd</span> tests/tests_integration
pytest
</pre></div>
</div>
</section>
<section id="usage">
<h2>Usage<a class="headerlink" href="#usage" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>To get started, browse the Jupyter notebooks in the examples/ directory in this
repository.</p>
<p>Once you understand the workflow, read the Module Documentation below to
learn more about paperfetcher’s various modules, classes and functions.</p>
</section>
<section id="the-team">
<h2>The Team<a class="headerlink" href="#the-team" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>Paperfetcher was developed by <a class="reference external" href="https://apallath.github.io">Akash Pallath</a> at the <a class="reference external" href="https://www.upenn.edu">University of Pennsylvania</a> and <a class="reference external" href="https://qiyangzh.github.io">Qiyang Zhang</a> at the <a class="reference external" href="https://www.jhu.edu">Johns Hopkins University</a>.</p>

  <section id="paperfetcher-package">
<h1>paperfetcher package<a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher-package" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h1>
<section id="submodules">
<h2>Submodules<a class="headerlink" href="#submodules" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
</section>
<section id="module-paperfetcher.apiclients">
<span id="paperfetcher-apiclients-module"></span><h2>paperfetcher.apiclients module<a class="headerlink" href="#module-paperfetcher.apiclients" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>Client implementations to communicate with various APIs.</p>
<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.apiclients.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">COCIQuery</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">components</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">query_params</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query" title="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.apiclients.Query</span></code></a></p>
<p>Class for structuring and executing COCI REST API queries.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>components</strong> (<em>collections.OrderedDict</em>) – Components to append to the base URL.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>query_params</strong> (<em>collections.OrderedDict</em>) – Ordered dictionary of query parameters.</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.components">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">components</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.components" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Components to append to the base URL.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>collections.OrderedDict</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.query_base">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">query_base</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.query_base" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Base URL for query (<a class="reference external" href="https://opencitations.net/index/coci/api">https://opencitations.net/index/coci/api</a>/v{}/…).</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>str</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.query_params">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">query_params</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.query_params" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Dictionary of query parameters.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>collections.OrderedDict</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.headers">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">headers</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.headers" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Dictionary of HTTP headers.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>dict</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.response">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">response</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.COCIQuery.response" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Response recieved on executing GET query to the COCI API.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>requests.Response</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>Querying the references of a paper with a known DOI:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">COCIQuery</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">components</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">OrderedDict</span><span class="p">([(</span><span class="s2">&quot;references&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;10.1021/acs.jpcb.1c02191&quot;</span><span class="p">)]))</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">json</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="go">[{&#39;oci&#39;: &#39;020010002013610122837192512113701120002010901-0200100000236191212370201090809&#39;, &#39;creation&#39;: &#39;2021-05-12&#39;, &#39;timespan&#39;: &#39;P9Y5M19D&#39;,</span>
<span class="go">...}]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Querying the citations of a paper with a known DOI:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">COCIQuery</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">components</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="n">OrderedDict</span><span class="p">([(</span><span class="s2">&quot;citations&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;10.1021/acs.jpcb.1c02191&quot;</span><span class="p">)]))</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">json</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="go">[{&#39;oci&#39;: &#39;0200100000236252421370200020100050206-020010002013610122837192512113701120002010901&#39;, &#39;creation&#39;: &#39;2021-10-02&#39;, &#39;timespan&#39;: &#39;P4M20D&#39;,</span>
<span class="go"> &#39;journal_sc&#39;: &#39;no&#39;, &#39;author_sc&#39;: &#39;no&#39;, &#39;citing&#39;: &#39;10.1002/pol.20210526&#39;, &#39;cited&#39;: &#39;10.1021/acs.jpcb.1c02191&#39;}]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.apiclients.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">CrossrefQuery</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">components</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">query_params</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query" title="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.apiclients.Query</span></code></a></p>
<p>Class for structuring and executing Crossref REST API queries.</p>
<p>Query components can be added to the base URL by passing an ordered dictionary to the components argument.
For example, the components dictionary <cite>{“comp1-key”: “comp1-value”, “comp2”: None}</cite> changes
the query URL to <cite>https://api.crossref.org/comp1-key/comp1-value/comp2/</cite>.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>components</strong> (<em>collections.OrderedDict</em>) – Components to append to the base URL.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>query_params</strong> (<em>collections.OrderedDict</em>) – Ordered dictionary of query parameters.</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery.components">

</dd></dl>

<dl class="field-list simple">

</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery.headers">

<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>

</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery.response">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">response</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.CrossrefQuery.response" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Response recieved on executing GET query to the Crossref API.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>requests.Response</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>Querying the metadata of a paper with a known DOI:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">CrossrefQuery</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">components</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="s2">&quot;works&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="s2">&quot;10.1021/acs.jpcb.1c02191&quot;</span><span class="p">})</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">json</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="go">{&#39;status&#39;: &#39;ok&#39;, &#39;message-type&#39;: &#39;work&#39;, &#39;message-version&#39;: &#39;1.0.0&#39;,</span>
<span class="go">&#39;message&#39;: {...}}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>Query to fetch all articles from a journal with a known ISSN:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">components</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">OrderedDict</span><span class="p">([(</span><span class="s2">&quot;journals&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;1520-5126&quot;</span><span class="p">),</span>
<span class="go">                          (&quot;works&quot;, None)])</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">CrossrefQuery</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">components</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">json</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="go">{&#39;status&#39;: &#39;ok&#39;, &#39;message-type&#39;: &#39;work&#39;, &#39;message-version&#39;: &#39;1.0.0&#39;,</span>
<span class="go">&#39;message&#39;: {...}}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.apiclients.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">Query</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">base_url</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">None</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">query_params</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">dict</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">headers</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">str</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">{}</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">object</span></code></p>
<p>Base class for structuring and executing HTTP GET queries.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>base_url</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Base URL for query (such as api.xyz.com/get).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>query_params</strong> (<em>dict</em>) – Dictionary of query parameters.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>headers</strong> (<em>dict</em>) – Dictionary of HTTP headers to pass along with the query.</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.query_base">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">query_base</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.query_base" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Base URL for query (such as api.xyz.com/get).</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>str</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.query_params">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">query_params</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.query_params" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Dictionary of query parameters.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>dict</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">

<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>

</dd>

</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.response">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">response</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.apiclients.Query.response" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Response recieved on executing GET query.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>requests.Response</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>A simple Query to the Github REST API:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">Query</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;https://api.github.com&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>A Query to the Github REST API to fetch a list of all public repositories in the paperfetcher organization:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">Query</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;https://api.github.com/orgs/paperfetcher/repos&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>              <span class="n">query_params</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="s2">&quot;type&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="s2">&quot;public&quot;</span><span class="p">},</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>              <span class="n">headers</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">{</span><span class="s2">&quot;Accept&quot;</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="s2">&quot;application/vnd.github.v3+json&quot;</span><span class="p">})</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">query</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">response</span>
<span class="go">&lt;Response [200]&gt;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<dl class="py property">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="id0">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">property</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">response</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#id0" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd></dd></dl>

</dd></dl>

</section>
<section id="module-paperfetcher.datastructures">
<span id="paperfetcher-datastructures-module"></span><h2>paperfetcher.datastructures module<a class="headerlink" href="#module-paperfetcher.datastructures" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>Custom data structures for paperfetcher.</p>
<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">CitationsDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">field_names</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">tuple</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset" title="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset</span></code></a></p>
<p>Stores a tabular dataset of citations, with multiple custom fields.</p>
<p>CitationsDatasets can be exported to pandas DataFrames, and loaded from or saved to disk
in text, CSV, or Excel file formats.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>field_names</strong> (<em>tuple</em>) – Names (str) of fields.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>items</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of citations to store (default=[]). Each citation should be an iterable of length len(field_names).</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>To create a CitationsDataset object to store the DOI, URL, article title, authors, and date of issue for each citation:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">field_names</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;DOI&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;URL&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;title&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;author&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;issued&quot;</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">data</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[[</span><span class="s2">&quot;10.xxyy/0.0.0.000001&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000001&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;A study of A&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;P, Q and R&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;2020-02-20&quot;</span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>        <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;10.xxyy/0.0.0.000002&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000002&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;An investigation into B&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;Q, R and P&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;2020-03-20&quot;</span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>        <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;10.xxyy/0.0.0.000003&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000003&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;The causes of C in D&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;R, P and Q&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;2020-04-20&quot;</span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>        <span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;10.xxyy/0.0.0.000004&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000004&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;Characterizing the role of E on F&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;P and Q&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;2020-05-20&quot;</span><span class="p">]]</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">CitationsDataset</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">field_names</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">data</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To add a citation to the CitationsDataset:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="s2">&quot;10.xxyy/0.0.0.000005&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000005&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;The G effect&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;Q and P&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;2020-06-20&quot;</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To export the DOIDataset object to a pandas DataFrame:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">df</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_df</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">df</span>
<span class="go">                    DOI                                      URL                              title      author      issued</span>
<span class="go">0  10.xxyy/0.0.0.000001  https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000001                       A study of A  P, Q and R  2020-02-20</span>
<span class="go">1  10.xxyy/0.0.0.000002  https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000002            An investigation into B  Q, R and P  2020-03-20</span>
<span class="go">2  10.xxyy/0.0.0.000003  https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000003               The causes of C in D  R, P and Q  2020-04-20</span>
<span class="go">3  10.xxyy/0.0.0.000004  https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000004  Characterizing the role of E on F     P and Q  2020-05-20</span>
<span class="go">4  10.xxyy/0.0.0.000005  https://dx.doi.org/10.xxyy/0.0.0.000005                       The G effect     Q and P  2020-06-20</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To save data to disk:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_txt</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;cits.txt&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;cits.csv&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_excel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;cits.xlsx&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.append">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">append</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">item</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.append" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Adds a citation to the dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.extend">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">extend</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.extend" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Adds each citation from a list of citations (i.e. eacher inner list of nested list) to the dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_csv">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">save_csv</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">file</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_csv" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Saves dataset to .csv file. The first row of the CSV file contains field names.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_excel">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">save_excel</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">file</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_excel" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Saves dataset to Excel file (uses Pandas). The first row of the Excel file contains field names.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_txt">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">save_txt</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">file</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.save_txt" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Saves dataset to .txt file.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.to_df">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">to_df</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.CitationsDataset.to_df" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Converts dataset to DataFrame.</p>
</dd></dl>

</dd></dl>

<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">DOIDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset" title="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset</span></code></a></p>
<p>Stores a dataset of DOIs.</p>
<p>DOIDatasets can be exported to pandas DataFrames, and loaded from or saved to disk
in text, CSV, or Excel file formats.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p><strong>items</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of DOIs (str) to store (default=[]).</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>To create a DOIDataset object from a list of DOIs:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">DOIDataset</span><span class="p">([</span><span class="s2">&quot;x1.y1.z1/123123&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;x2.y2.z2/456456&quot;</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To add a DOI to the DOIDataset object:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">append</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;x3.y3.z3/789789&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To export the DOIDataset object to a pandas DataFrame:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">df</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">to_df</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">df</span>
<span class="go">               DOI</span>
<span class="go">0  x1.y1.z1/123123</span>
<span class="go">1  x2.y2.z2/456456</span>
<span class="go">3  x3.y3.z3/789789</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To save data to disk:</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_txt</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;dois.txt&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_csv</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;dois.csv&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">save_excel</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s2">&quot;dois.xlsx&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset.extend_dataset">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">extend_dataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">ds</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset" title="paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset</span></a></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.DOIDataset.extend_dataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Appends all items from DOIDataset ds to the end of the current dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">Dataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">object</span></code></p>
<p>Abstract interface that defines functions for child Dataset classes to implement.</p>
<p>Datasets are designed to store [usually tabular] data (as input to or output from paperfetcher searches), export
data to pandas DataFrames, and load/save data to disk using common data formats (txt, csv, xlsx).</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p><strong>items</strong> (<em>iterable</em>) – Items to store in dataset (default=[]).</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset.append">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">append</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">item</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset.append" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Adds an item to the dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset.extend">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">extend</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset.extend" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Adds each item from a list of items to the dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.HeadlessRISWriter.PATTERN">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">PATTERN</span></span><em class="property"><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="pre">str</span></em><em class="property"><span class="w"> </span><span class="p"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="pre">'{tag}</span>&#160; <span class="pre">-</span> <span class="pre">{value}'</span></em><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.HeadlessRISWriter.PATTERN" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>

<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">RISDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">items</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset" title="paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset"><code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.Dataset</span></code></a></p>
<p>Stores a dataset of RIS items. RIS items are rispy-readable dictionaries.</p>
<p>An RISDataset can be created from an RIS-formatted string, or from an RIS file.
An RISDataset can be written to an RIS-formatted string, or to an RIS file.
Individual item dictionaries in an RISDataset can be modified to add new tags or change
the values of existing tags.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p><strong>items</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of citations to store (default=[]). Each citation should be a rispy-readable dictionary.</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p class="rubric">Examples</p>
<p>To create an RISDataset from a list of rispy-readable dictionaries (see rispy doc on GitHub for details):</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">dict_list</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="p">[{</span><span class="s1">&#39;journal_name&#39;</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="o">...</span><span class="p">},</span> <span class="p">{</span><span class="s1">&#39;journal_name: ...&#39;</span><span class="p">},</span> <span class="o">...</span><span class="p">]</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">RISDataset</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">dict_list</span><span class="p">)</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>To load an RISDataset from an RIS-formatted string:
&gt;&gt;&gt; ds = RISDataset.from_ris_string(ris_string)</p>
<p>To load an RISDataset from an RIS file:
&gt;&gt;&gt; ds = RISDataset.from_ris(ris_file)</p>
<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset.extend_dataset">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">extend_dataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">ds</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><a class="reference internal" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset" title="paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset</span></a></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.datastructures.RISDataset.extend_dataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Appends all items from RISDataset ds to the end of the current dataset.</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">

<section id="module-paperfetcher.handsearch">
<span id="paperfetcher-handsearch-module"></span><h2>paperfetcher.handsearch module<a class="headerlink" href="#module-paperfetcher.handsearch" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>Classes to fetch all journal works (articles) matching a set of keywords and
within a given date range by querying various APIs.</p>
<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.handsearch.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">CrossrefSearch</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">ISSN</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">''</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">type</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">'journal-article'</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">keyword_list</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">None</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">from_date</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">None</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">until_date</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">None</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">batch_size</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">20</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">sort_order</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">'desc'</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">object</span></code></p>
<p>Retrieves all works from a journal, given its ISSN, which match
a set of keywords and are within a date range.</p>
<p>Calling a CrossrefSearch object performs the search. A search object can be
called with the arguments <cite>display_progress_bar</cite> (True/False; default=True) to
toggle the display of a search progress bar, <cite>select</cite> (True/False; default=False), and
<cite>select_fields</cite> (list) to query only a subset of metadata for each journal
article.</p>
<p>If select is False, a full (memory and time intensive) search is performed,
fetching all metadata associated with each journal work.</p>
<p>If select is True, a subset of fields to fetch can be specified using the
select_fields parameter. Check the Crossref REST API doc
for details on which field names are permissible.</p>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Note</p>
<p>Performing a search with no keywords and select=False can be very time- and memory- intensive.
The search object will complain when such a search is performed.</p>
</div>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>ISSN</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Journal (web) ISSN.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>type</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Type of works to fetch (default=”journal-article”).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>keyword_list</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of keywords (str) to query with (default=None).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>from_date</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Fetch articles published from (and after) this date (format=”YYYY-MM-DD”, default=None).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>until_date</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Fetch articles published until this date (format=”YYYY-MM-DD”, default=None).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>batch_size</strong> (<em>int</em>) – Number of works to fetch in each batch (default=20).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>sort_order</strong> (<em>str</em>) – Order in which to sort works by date (“asc” or “desc”, default=”desc”).</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.ISSN">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">ISSN</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.ISSN" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Journal (web) ISSN.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>str</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<p class="rubric">Examples</p>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.dry_run">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">dry_run</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">select</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">False</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">select_fields</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.dry_run" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>How many works will this search fetch?</p>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_CitationsDataset">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">get_CitationsDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">field_list</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">field_parsers_list</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_CitationsDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Parses a selection of fields from search results and returns them as a CitationsDataset object.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>field_list</strong> (<em>list</em>) – Names of fields to parse (see Crossref REST API doc for permissible field name values).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>field_parsers_list</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of field parser functions corresponding to each field name. A <cite>None</cite> value means that no parser
is needed for that field.</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
<dt class="field-even">Returns</dt>
<dd class="field-even"><p>CitationsDataset</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<p class="rubric">Example</p>
<div class="doctest highlight-default notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">handsearch</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">CrossrefSearch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">ISSN</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">&quot;1520-5126&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">keyword_list</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s2">&quot;hydration&quot;</span><span class="p">],</span> <span class="n">from_date</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">&quot;2018-01-01&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>                                   <span class="n">until_date</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s2">&quot;2020-01-01&quot;</span><span class="p">)</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">select</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">select_fields</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">&#39;DOI&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;URL&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;title&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;author&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;issued&#39;</span><span class="p">])</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">ds</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">search</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">get_CitationsDataset</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">field_list</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="s1">&#39;DOI&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;URL&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;title&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;author&#39;</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s1">&#39;issued&#39;</span><span class="p">],</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>                                 <span class="n">field_parsers_list</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="p">[</span><span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">parsers</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">crossref_title_parser</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="gp">... </span>                                                     <span class="n">parsers</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">crossref_authors_parser</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">parsers</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">crossref_date_parser</span><span class="p">])</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_DOIDataset">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">get_DOIDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_DOIDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Extracts DOIs from search results and returns them as a DOIDataset object.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Returns</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>DOIDataset</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py method">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_RISDataset">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">get_RISDataset</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">extra_field_list</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">extra_field_parser_list</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em>, <em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">extra_field_rispy_tags</span></span><span class="o"><span class="pre">=</span></span><span class="default_value"><span class="pre">[]</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.handsearch.CrossrefSearch.get_RISDataset" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Extracts DOIs from search results and fetches RIS data for each DOI using
Crossref’s content negotiation service.</p>
<p>Extra fields in the search results that are not automatically populated
by Crossref’s content negotation service can be mapped to the RIS format (through rispy’s mapping)
using the <cite>extra_fields</cite>, <cite>extra_field_parser_list</cite>, and <cite>extra_field_rispy_tags</cite>
arguments.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><ul class="simple">
<li><p><strong>extra_field_list</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of extra fields to parse and include in RIS file (see Crossref REST API doc for permissible field name values).</p></li>
<li><p><strong>extra_field_parser_list</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of field parser functions corresponding to each extra field name. A <cite>None</cite> value means that no parser
is needed for that field.</p></li>
<li><p><strong>extra_field_rispy_tags</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of rispy tags for each extra field.</p></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

</dd></dl>

</section>

<section id="module-paperfetcher.snowballsearch">
<span id="paperfetcher-snowballsearch-module"></span><h2>paperfetcher.snowballsearch module<a class="headerlink" href="#module-paperfetcher.snowballsearch" title="Permalink to this headline"></a></h2>
<p>Classes to fetch all journal articles in the references of (i.e. backward search)
or citing (i.e. forward search) a set of journal articles.</p>
<p>For backward search, you can use either Crossref or COCI (should be equivalent).
For forward search, you can only use COCI at the moment.</p>
<dl class="py class">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.snowballsearch.COCIBackwardReferenceSearch">
<em class="property"><span class="pre">class</span><span class="w"> </span></em><span class="sig-prename descclassname"><span class="pre">paperfetcher.snowballsearch.</span></span><span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">COCIBackwardReferenceSearch</span></span><span class="sig-paren">(</span><em class="sig-param"><span class="n"><span class="pre">search_dois</span></span><span class="p"><span class="pre">:</span></span><span class="w"> </span><span class="n"><span class="pre">list</span></span></em><span class="sig-paren">)</span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.snowballsearch.COCIBackwardReferenceSearch" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>Bases: <code class="xref py py-class docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">object</span></code></p>
<p>Retrieves the (DOIs of) all articles in the references of a list of (DOIs of) articles
by using the COCI REST API.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Parameters</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p><strong>search_dois</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of DOIs (str) to fetch references of.</p>
</dd>
</dl>
<dl class="py attribute">
<dt class="sig sig-object py" id="paperfetcher.snowballsearch.COCIBackwardReferenceSearch.search_dois">
<span class="sig-name descname"><span class="pre">search_dois</span></span><a class="headerlink" href="#paperfetcher.snowballsearch.COCIBackwardReferenceSearch.search_dois" title="Permalink to this definition"></a></dt>
<dd><p>List of DOIs (str) to fetch references of.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>list</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<dl class="py attribute">

<dd><p>Set of DOIs which are referenced by the DOIs in <cite>search_dois</cite>.</p>
<dl class="field-list simple">
<dt class="field-odd">Type</dt>
<dd class="field-odd"><p>set</p>
</dd>
</dl>
</dd></dl>

<p class="rubric">Example</p>

<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span><span class="p">()</span>
<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="nb">len</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">search</span><span class="p">)</span>

<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">result_dois</span>
<span class="go">{&#39;10.1021/jp972543+&#39;, &#39;10.1073/pnas.0708088105&#39;,  ... ,  &#39;10.1073/pnas.0705830104&#39;}</span>
</pre></div>
</div>

<dd class="field-odd"><p><strong>search_dois</strong> (<em>list</em>) – List of DOIs (str) to fetch references of.</p>
</dd>
</dl>

<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span><span class="p">()</span>

<span class="gp">&gt;&gt;&gt; </span><span class="n">search</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">result_dois</span>
<span class="go">{&#39;10.1021/jp972543+&#39;, &#39;10.1073/pnas.0708088105&#39;,  ... ,  &#39;10.1073/pnas.0705830104&#39;}</span>

FILE: docs/_sources/index.rst.txt

.. paperfetcher documentation master file, created by
   sphinx-quickstart on Sat Sep  4 16:24:49 2021.
   You can adapt this file completely to your liking, but it should at least
   contain the root `toctree` directive.

###############################
paperfetcher
###############################

:Release: |release|

Paperfetcher is a Python package to automate handsearching and citation searching
(snowballing) for systematic reviews. To learn more about Paperfetcher, visit
`paperfetcher.github.io`_.

.. _`paperfetcher.github.io`: https://paperfetcher.github.io/

Paperfetcher works with Python 3.7+.

You can either install paperfetcher using `pip`_, or alternatively, clone the source code from
Github and install it from source.

.. _`pip`: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/

Installation with pip
*********************

1. `Install pip`_

.. _`Install pip`: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/getting-started/

2. Install paperfetcher using pip

Open a terminal window and run

.. code-block:: bash

  pip install paperfetcher

**OR**

Installation from source (requires git)
***************************************

**Source code** is available from
`https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher`_

1. Obtain the sources with `git`_

.. code-block:: bash

  git clone https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher.git

.. _`https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher`: https://github.com/paperfetcher/paperfetcher
.. _git: https://git-scm.com/

2. Install package

Open a terminal window in the paperfetcher directory and run

.. code-block:: bash

  python setup.py install


Running tests (if installed from source)
**************************

You can run tests to make sure that paperfetcher is working correctly on your system.

To run unit tests:

.. code-block:: bash

  cd tests/tests_unit
  pytest

To run integration tests:

.. code-block:: bash

  cd tests/tests_integration
  pytest

Usage
**************************

To get started, browse the Jupyter notebooks in the examples/ directory in this
repository.

Once you understand the workflow, read the Module Documentation below to
learn more about paperfetcher's various modules, classes and functions.


The Team
******************

Paperfetcher was developed by `Akash Pallath`_ at the `University of Pennsylvania`_ and `Qiyang Zhang`_ at the `Johns Hopkins University`_.

.. _`Akash Pallath`: https://apallath.github.io
.. _`University of Pennsylvania`: https://www.upenn.edu
.. _`Qiyang Zhang`: https://qiyangzh.github.io
.. _`Johns Hopkins University`: https://www.jhu.edu

========================================

.. toctree::
   :maxdepth: 2
   :caption: Module documentation:

   paperfetcher

========================================

Indices and tables
==================

* :ref:`genindex`
* :ref:`modindex`
* :ref:`search`
